models.journalEntry: A personal study note with content, type classification, multi-anchors, system theme tags, user tags, and study context.
models.anchor: A link from a journal entry to a passage, character, theme, or narrative unit.
models.userTag: A personal taxonomy label created by the user.
models.collection: A curated group of passages, characters, themes, and notes assembled for a purpose.
models.collectionItem: An item within a collection with position and annotation.
models.bookmark: A lightweight verse marker for quick retrieval.

relationships: JournalEntry may have related entries via shared anchors/themes/tags
Collection has many CollectionItems in user-defined order

entries.content: Why does the text say Jesus 'had to' pass through Samaria? Was it geographical necessity or theological intention? Most Jews took the longer route around Samaria. The 'had to' feels deliberate — like the narrative is telling us this meeting wasn't accidental.

She came at noon because she didn't want to be seen. I've done that — chosen the wrong time just to avoid people. There's something about invisibility that feels like protection until someone sees you and it turns out that's what you needed.

Forgiveness isn't a feeling. It's a decision you make before the feeling catches up. Joseph had years between the pit and the palace, and the forgiveness happened somewhere in those years — not at the dramatic moment of revelation in Genesis 45, but in the quiet accumulation of choices not to become bitter.

The Greek word for 'living' in 'living water' (zaō) literally means flowing as opposed to still. She heard plumbing. He meant eternity. The entire conversation is two people using the same words to mean completely different things — and the breakthrough happens when she stops hearing literally.
Help me see the people I've been walking past. The ones who come at noon because they don't want to be seen. The ones whose names nobody remembers.
Esau's cry in v.34 — 'Bless me — me too, my father!' — is the rawest moment in Genesis. He's not angry yet. He's just heartbroken.

collections.title: Comfort passages for hospital visits
collections.description: Verses and reflections I reach for when visiting someone in the hospital. Not platitudes — real comfort.

collections.title: Verses my daughter asked about
collections.description: Questions she's brought to the dinner table, with the passages and my notes on how we talked through them.

collections.title: Forgiveness series — source material
collections.description: Everything I've collected for the sermon series. Overlaps with the Study Builder project but includes personal reflections too.
